message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature: add strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. These are the two files my task touches. What files does your feature need? If it also touches ImageOps.py we need to split by function/section or take turns.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Only touching exif_transpose function and its test. Please propose your files so we can confirm disjoint edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Both our features modify the same exif_transpose function/signature in the same two files, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposal: sequential turn-based editing instead. I'll go first: add my strip_metadata param + test, then send DONE. You then read the updated file, add your transform_callback param alongside mine (don't remove/rename my param or test), add your own test, then send your own DONE. Reply ACCEPT if OK with me going first, or REVISE if you'd rather go first.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need to touch exif_transpose in the same file, so a file-level split won't work. Propose sequencing instead: you go first (add strip_metadata param + tests), send DONE when finished. I'll then pull the current file state, add my transform_callback param as an additional independent optional kwarg (won't touch your param's logic), and append my own test functions/cases without modifying yours. If you'd rather go second, say so and I'll go first instead - just let me know so we don't edit concurrently.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Accepted. You go first: add strip_metadata param + tests, send DONE when finished. I'll then add transform_callback alongside your param without touching your code, add my own tests, and send my own DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed - I'll go first, add strip_metadata param + tests to exif_transpose, then send DONE. You then add transform_callback on top without touching my param/tests.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished: added strip_metadata keyword-only param to exif_transpose (both overloads + impl), plus 5 new tests (test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata, _no_orientation, _in_place, _in_place_no_orientation, test_exif_transpose_keeps_metadata_by_default). All 39 tests in Tests/test_imageops.py pass. Existing signature/behavior unchanged when strip_metadata is omitted (defaults False). Go ahead and add your transform_callback param on top - just re-read the file first since line numbers shifted from my edits.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Finished: added keyword-only transform_callback param to exif_transpose (both overloads + impl). It takes the orientation int and returns a Transpose method, a sequence of methods (applied in order), or None to skip transforming - overriding the default mapping when provided. Added 4 new tests (test_exif_transpose_transform_callback, _sequence, _none, _in_place). Note: I did not see your strip_metadata edits in my working tree when I started (it was clean) - looks like we're in separate sandboxes/copies, so no clobbering occurred on my end. All 38 tests in Tests/test_imageops.py pass in my copy. Wrote final diff to patch.txt.
